other_observers: Brandon Percival; We both saw it fly out of the sedge meadow into the bullrushes.
specific_location: Upper Twin Pond, Chico Basin Ranch, wet sedge meadow.
description: This was a tan and light brown wren with a whitish throat. The bill was short, with light lower mandible and curved slightly downward. The tail and wings were heavily bared with buff and dark brown bars. The back had fine light streaks. The crown was without a strong pattern. Overall slender and small.Behaviors: Flew out of knee-high sedges into the edge of 6' tall bullrushes where it chipped and skulked. I went into the rushes and it came as close as 6 feet where I could look down on it without binoculars.Call: sharp chip notes from hiding spot in dense bullrushesPlumage: unknown
